title: gbfs-od-reconstruction: Standard compliance bounds origin–destination identifiability in GBFS bike-sharing feeds
description: Companion software, data, and LaTeX source for the manuscript 'Standard compliance bounds origin–destination identifiability in GBFS bike-sharing feeds: a cost-learning and sampling-horizon analysis' (R. Fossé and G. Pallares, in preparation for Computer Standards & Interfaces). The repository provides: (i) an identifiability characterisation of the entropic-OT/gravity cost learned from an identifier-tracked micro-sample (identifiable only modulo additive station effects); (ii) a selection-bias decomposition proving that station-emptiness censoring cancels while 60-second polling aliasing does not, and a sampling-horizon law T* scaling as δ⁻⁴ and 1/q with a standard-compliance threshold; (iii) a feed-by-feed audit protocol and pilot that measures the five governing constants (q, κ, β, γ, Λ). Each numbered experiment script reproduces a specific table or figure.
notes: Companion software release for the Computer Standards & Interfaces submission. The git tag 'v1.0' marks the snapshot submitted. Reproducibility: SEED=42 master seed, dependencies pinned in requirements.txt, Python 3.12.
